revision.text:
[[ja:走水神社 (横須賀市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hashirimizu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Hashirimizu Jinja 14.jpg|250px|Hashirimizu Shrine（April 2, 2011）]]
Main Shrine
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|15|41.2|N|139|43|51.3|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Yokosuka|en|Yokosuka|qid=Q200578|lt=Yokosuka City|1=Yokosuka}}, {{ill|Hashirimizu|ja|走水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashirimizu|1=Hashirimizu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q122197930}} 2-12-5 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 15 | Latitude seconds = 41.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 51.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | Map Country Code = JP | deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}}, {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}} | type = | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = | alternate_name = | festival = October 15 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Kanagawa | Label Position = left }} '''Hashirimizu Shrine''' (走水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hashirimizu|ja|走水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashirimizu|1=Hashirimizu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q122197930}}, {{ill|Yokosuka|en|Yokosuka|qid=Q200578|lt=Yokosuka City|1=Yokosuka}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, near the eastern edge of the {{ill|Miura Peninsula|en|Miura Peninsula|qid=Q536757|lt=Miura Peninsula|1=Miura Peninsula}}, close to {{ill|Tokyo Bay|en|Tokyo Bay|qid=Q141017|lt=Tokyo Bay|1=Tokyo Bay}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}} * {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}} (enshrined in 1909) == History == The precise date of the shrine's foundation is unknown due to losses from {{ill|Conflagration|en|Conflagration|qid=Q168983|lt=fires|1=Conflagration}} and other events. According to tradition, during {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}}'s eastern expedition, he crossed the {{ill|Uraga Channel|en|Uraga Channel|qid=Q723431|lt=Uraga Channel|1=Uraga Channel}} from this location. Before setting out, he reportedly gave his crown to local villagers, who buried it in a stone chest and built a shrine over it. While the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} mention Yamato Takeru's journey from {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|qid=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province|1=Sagami Province}} to {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|qid=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province|1=Kazusa Province}}, they do not specify precise locations.‘Nihon Shoki’ lists only places like Yaizu (Shizuoka), Ashiura (Kamogawa, Chiba), and Tamaura (Kujukuri Coast).In historical texts, "Hashirimizu" refers to a route across the Uraga Channel rather than a specific place. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} also recounts how {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, during his tour of the eastern provinces, crossed the "淡水門" (a reference to the channel).‘Nihon Shoki’ states, “In winter, Keikō arrived in Kazusa, crossed the channel to reach ‘淡水門’...” It is inferred that Yamato Takeru likely passed through this area during his own eastern expedition.‘Tokyo Bay History’ p. 84. As Yamato Takeru set sail for Kazusa Province, a violent storm arose. To calm the waters, Princess Ototachibana sacrificed herself by leaping into the sea. Days later, her comb washed ashore, and villagers built a shrine at Flag Hill (Goshogasaki) to enshrine it. This shrine, originally called Tachibana Shrine, was relocated to the grounds of Hashirimizu Shrine in {{ill|1885|en|1885|qid=Q7821|lt=1885|1=1885}}, after the site was repurposed for military use, and later incorporated into Hashirimizu Shrine in {{ill|1909|en|1909|qid=Q2057|lt=1909|1=1909}}. == Grounds == * {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|qid=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}} * Three shrines (Suga Shrine, Shinmei Shrine, Suwa Shrine) * {{ill|Separate Shrine|ja|別宮|lt=Betsugū|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Separate Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Dedicated to the maidservants of Princess Ototachibana. * {{ill|Water Shrine|ja|水神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Water Shrine|1=Suijinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55522725}} (Kappa Daimyōjin): Honoring kappa folklore from Hashirimizu. * Memorial Monument to Princess Ototachibana: Erected in {{ill|1910|en|1910|qid=Q2075|lt=1910|1=1910}} by {{ill|Tōgō Heihachirō|en|Tōgō Heihachirō|qid=Q296785|lt=Tōgō Heihachirō|1=Tōgō Heihachirō}}, {{ill|Nogi Maresuke|en|Nogi Maresuke|qid=Q468122|lt=Nogi Maresuke|1=Nogi Maresuke}}, and others, with an inscription by Princess Masako. * Mechanical Torpedo: A Russian torpedo captured during the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|qid=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War|1=Russo-Japanese War}}, donated in 1910. * Knife Monument: Built in 1973, referencing the story of Otomo no Kurotomo serving Yamato Takeru. * Rudder Monument: Erected in 1975 to pray for maritime safety, inspired by Princess Ototachibana's sacrifice. == Festivals == * {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|qid=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai|1=Kinensai}}: Early March * Summer Festival (Tenno Festival): Second Saturday in July * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}}: October 15 == Access == * Address: 2-12-5 Hashirimizu, Yokosuka City, Kanagawa Prefecture * Nearest bus stop: "Hashirimizu Shrine" on {{ill|Keihin Kyuko Bus|en|Keihin Kyuko Bus|qid=Q11374532|lt=Keikyu Bus|1=Keihin Kyuko Bus}} (S24, Hori24 routes) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Toshio Kikuchi, ‘Tokyo Bay History,’ Dainihon Tosho Insatsu, 1974. == Related Works == * {{cite book|和書|title=Dainippon Chishi Taikei|volume=Volume 40 {{ill|Shinpen Sagami Province Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編相模国風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Sagami Province Fudoki Manuscript|1=Shinpen Sagami no Kuni Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11503942}} 5巻之115村里部三浦郡巻之9|chapter=Kinukake Sho Hashirimizu Village Hashirimizu Gongen Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179240/165}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Province Fudoki Manuscript}}|publisher=Yuzankaku|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kanō Shrine|ja|叶神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanō Shrine|1=kanou Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11412213}}: Hashirimizu Shrine is managed as an affiliated shrine without permanent clergy, under the care of Kanō Shrine (Nishiuraga, Yokosuka City). * {{ill|Yamato Takeru#Shrines|ja|日本武尊ゆかりの神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru#Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Cape Kannon|en|Cape Kannon|qid=Q11630643|lt=Cape Kannon|1=Cape Kannon}} * {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|en|Uraga, Kanagawa|qid=Q2735604|lt=Uraga|1=Uraga, Kanagawa}} == External Links == * [http://www12.plala.or.jp/hasirimizujinjya/ Hashirimizu Shrine] (Official Website) * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=452&cd=1205065&scd=&npg=0 Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Commonscat|Hashirimizu Jinja|走水神社}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|走水神社 (横須賀市)|version=104562154|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:走水神社]] [[ja:走水神社 (横須賀市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11636076}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yokosuka, Kanagawa]] [[Category:History of Yokosuka]] [[Category:Ototachibana-hime]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Uraga]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Ōtsu, Yokosuka]]
